items.title: Sixteen-year-old in custody suspected of killing mother, injuring little sister

children.content: WA Police say a 16-year-old boy is in custody suspected of stabbing his mother to death and seriously injuring his 12-year-old sister in an alleged family violence homicide in Perth's south-east.

items.title: Former Catholic bishop sexually abused boys at 'shirts-off' parties, court hears

children.content: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: An elderly man wearing a light grey flat cap and black coat leavs court.

items.title: 'Don't go there': Hanson fires up in Perth over White Australia policy

children.content: Pauline Hanson angrily denies suggestions her strident opposition to migration by anyone from "radical Islamic countries" amounts to a version of the White Australia policy.

image.altText: Pauline is pictured at a lectern advertising the Leadership Matters event against a blue background.

items.title: Police probe use of force on handcuffed teen injured during Perth arrest

children.content: A handcuffed teenager was injured when he was forced to the ground by a police officer, sparking a WA Police review after video of the incident emerged.

items.title: Failings behind one of WA's worst sex abuse scandals laid bare by whistleblower

children.content: As one of Western Australia's most monstrous serial paedophiles approaches the end of his prison term, the woman who helped bring his crimes to light is publishing her account of the story.

items.title: Rebel Wilson's defamation fight reaches judgment day

children.content: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson will learn today whether she defamed the lead actor on her "fun girl-power" film, in a judgment to be handed down in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court wearing a green jacket.

items.title: Labor members revive push for national gambling watchdog

children.content: Labor members and unions are gearing up for fights over gambling, gas and AI at the party's national conference later this week.

image.altText: A man's side profile in shadows with a phone close to his face

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

image.altText: Hamish MacDonald,  Allesandra Galloni, Hugh Marks, Michael Miller, Jane Palfreyman, Matt Stanton.

items.title: Who paid for Pauline Hanson's trip to Europe? Not taxpayers, she says

children.content: In her first appearance since returning to Australia, Pauline Hanson fields questions about her trip to Europe with billionaire Gina Rinehart.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson sits on stage talking during a question and answer session at a breakfact function, against a blue background.

items.title: Map reveals Australia's 'pharmacy deserts' are not all in the bush

children.content: Australia's "pharmacy deserts" have been mapped for the first time, revealing remote NT communities and outer-city Melbourne have fewer pharmacies per person than some other parts of the country.

gallery.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

thumbnail.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

items.title: New Ozploitation horror is like Puberty Blues from hell

children.content: After spending years in special effects and make-up, filmmaker Mia'Kate Russell's ocker nightmare Penny Lane Is Dead has made it to the silver screen.

image.altText: A man and a woman stand on a veranda

children.content: Where are macadamia nuts indigenous to? And what’s the capital city of Switzerland?

items.title: Stingers hitting new heights thanks to shared love for each other

children.content: Australia's women's water polo team, the Stingers, were the most successful Australian team at the Paris Olympics, winning a stunning silver medal. This is the secret to their success.

image.altText: Bronte Halligan, Sienna Green and Abby Andrews hug

items.title: Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash

children.content: AFL legend Tony Modra shares a post-crash update on social media, posting photos of him in hospital and with family, and saying he is now "getting out and about".

image.altText: Adelaide Crows legend Tony Modra with family.

items.title: UK YouTuber Yung Filly violently sexually assaulted young fan, court hears

children.content: A Perth court hears disturbing details of the YouTube personality's alleged sexual assault of a young woman who had a "celebrity crush" on him.

items.title: JackJumpers coach shares advice on how to win over Tasmanians

items.title: Woman critically injured after stabbing in Kalgoorlie

children.content: Part of the mining city's main street was blocked off and detectives are investigating after the woman was found with serious stab wounds.

items.title: Job system broken for workers, booming for private equity, report says

children.content: A new report argues Australia's employment services system is failing jobseekers, while filling the pockets of private equity firms and millionaires.

items.title: Five-year wait for much-needed housing in WA's holiday hotspot

children.content: A housing charity says bureaucratic delays have held up its attempt to build some small homes in Margaret River.

items.title: Decade of work helps remote island's native wildlife 'return to 1616'

children.content: Researchers were unafraid to get their hands "dirty" in an ambitious bid to restore WA's largest island to its pre-colonial state.
